name: machine-learning-ops-ml-pipeline
description: Design and implement a complete ML pipeline for: $ARGUMENTS
capabilities: Coordinates multiple specialized AI agents (data engineers, data scientists, ML engineers, MLOps engineers) to build ML pipelines
Provides guidance on data ingestion, feature engineering, and model training workflows
Generates infrastructure-as-code for Kubernetes deployments with Helm charts and Terraform modules
Configures experiment tracking with MLflow, Weights & Biases, and model registry integration
Sets up monitoring dashboards with Prometheus, Grafana, and automated drift detection alerts
Implements CI/CD pipelines for automated model validation and deployment with GitHub Actions
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only containing markdown content and prompt templates for ML pipeline orchestration. All static analysis findings were false positives: backticks at line 27 are markdown formatting for file references, 'blocker' matches are not cryptographic code, and 'reconnaissance' match is a configuration option label. No executable code, network calls, file operations, or secret handling detected.
valueStatement: Machine learning teams struggle to productionize models with proper infrastructure and monitoring. This skill orchestrates specialized AI agents to design complete ML pipelines from data engineering through deployment and continuous monitoring.
userTitle: Build Production ML Pipelines with Multi-Agent MLOps
limitations: Provides architectural guidance and configuration templates, not pre-built executable code
Requires access to cloud infrastructure or Kubernetes clusters for actual deployment
Does not include actual training data or pre-trained models
Configuration examples require customization for specific cloud providers and tools

useCases.title: Data Science Team Lead
useCases.description: Orchestrate end-to-end ML pipeline development for a new churn prediction model, coordinating data engineers for feature pipelines, data scientists for model experiments, and MLOps engineers for production deployment on Kubernetes.

useCases.title: ML Platform Engineer
useCases.description: Design and implement standardized MLOps infrastructure with experiment tracking, model registry, automated retraining pipelines, and comprehensive monitoring for multiple ML teams across the organization.

useCases.title: Startup CTO
useCases.description: Build production-ready ML serving infrastructure from scratch with cost optimization, auto-scaling, blue-green deployments, and observability to support a new recommendation engine feature.

promptTemplates.title: Quick ML Pipeline Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a basic ML pipeline for [use case] with data ingestion, model training, and API serving. Focus on essential components and provide a high-level architecture diagram.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner: Getting started with ML pipeline architecture

promptTemplates.title: Feature Store Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a feature store for [domain] using Feast. Include data source connections, feature definitions, transformation logic, online/offline store configuration, and integration with training and serving pipelines.

promptTemplates.title: Production Model Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete deployment strategy for a [model type] model on Kubernetes. Include KServe configuration, HPA autoscaling rules, Istio traffic management for canary releases, monitoring metrics, and rollback procedures.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced: Production deployment with high availability

promptTemplates.title: Full MLOps Platform
promptTemplates.prompt: Architect an enterprise MLOps platform for [organization size]. Cover multi-team experiment tracking with MLflow, centralized feature store, model registry with approval workflows, automated retraining triggers, drift detection, and cost allocation across teams.

bestPractices: Version control all pipeline artifacts including data schemas, model configurations, and infrastructure code using Git and DVC for reproducibility
Implement comprehensive validation gates at each pipeline stage: data quality checks, model performance thresholds, and infrastructure health checks before promotion
Design for observability from the start with structured logging, distributed tracing, and business metrics correlation to enable rapid incident response
antiPatterns: Training-serving skew: Using different feature computation logic in training versus production, leading to unexpected model performance degradation
Manual model deployment: Relying on manual processes for model promotion instead of automated CI/CD pipelines with validation gates
Monitoring blind spots: Only tracking system metrics without monitoring data drift, concept drift, or business KPI correlation

faq.question: What cloud providers does this skill support for ML pipeline deployment?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance for AWS, Azure, GCP, and multi-cloud deployments. Configuration examples include Terraform modules and Helm charts that can be adapted to any major cloud provider.

faq.question: Does this skill generate executable code or just documentation?
faq.answer: The skill provides detailed prompt templates for AI agents that generate implementation code, configuration files, and infrastructure-as-code. The output includes production-ready code snippets for critical components.

faq.question: How does the multi-agent orchestration work?
faq.answer: The skill coordinates specialized agents (data-engineer, data-scientist, ml-engineer, mlops-engineer, etc.) in a phased approach where each agent's output becomes input for subsequent phases, ensuring domain expertise at each step.

faq.question: What ML frameworks and tools are covered?
faq.answer: The skill covers popular tools including MLflow, Weights & Biases, Feast, Tecton, KServe, Seldon, Kubeflow, Airflow, FastAPI, and standard ML frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn.

faq.question: Can this skill help with model monitoring and drift detection?
faq.answer: Yes, the observability-engineer agent provides comprehensive monitoring setup including statistical drift detection (KS test, PSI), Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboards, and automated alerting with PagerDuty integration.

faq.question: Is this suitable for small teams or only enterprises?
faq.answer: The skill scales from simple single-model deployments to enterprise platforms. You can implement individual phases or components based on team size and requirements, starting with basic experiment tracking and growing to full orchestration.
